title: acmella oppositifolia
intro: Acmella oppositifolia, also known as the "buzz button" or "parlor tree," is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and analgesic effects. The plant is used to alleviate various health issues, such as toothaches, gum inflammation, and digestive problems, due to its ability to numb the affected area and reduce pain and swelling. Acmella oppositifolia is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or extract and can be used topically or taken orally to harness its health benefits.
